text.title: Content Differentiation and E-E-A-T
text.summary: What separates content that ranks from generic AI output: original research, first-hand experience, specific case studies, strong opinions, and E-E-A-T signals that AI cannot generate — these are the competitive moat when 77% of marketing teams use AI.

rule.statement: Every published piece must include at least two of: owned research or data, first-hand experience, expert quote or interview, specific case study with outcomes, or a contrarian opinion backed by evidence.
rule.rationale: Generic AI content aggregates the top 10 search results. Google's Helpful Content Updates actively deprioritize content without first-hand experience and original insight. Proprietary data elements are what AI cannot replicate and what drives 3x ranking advantage.

antipattern.description: Generic: "According to industry reports..." / "Best practices include..." / No specific numbers / No author perspective / No contrarian viewpoint.
antipattern.reason: This is what AI produces when given a topic without additional context. Every competitor's AI generates the same content. No E-E-A-T signals means Google treats it as low-quality.

example.note: Differentiated: specific data, first-hand experience, strong opinion.
example.code: "When we tested AI-only content across 50+ pieces in 2023, AI-only ranked with 2% CTR. AI + human insights: 7% CTR, 35% ranking improvement. Pieces with proprietary data ranked 3x better. My take: generic is dead."

rule.statement: Add E-E-A-T signals to every piece: Experience (I've tested this, our data shows), Expertise (credentials, methodology), Authoritativeness (primary sources, industry recognition), Trustworthiness (methodology transparency, honest failures).
rule.rationale: Google's E-E-A-T guidelines since 2023 explicitly reward first-hand experience. Content without these signals is algorithmically deprioritized regardless of keyword optimization. AI cannot generate genuine first-hand experience.

rule.statement: Define brand voice in a written guide with concrete examples before using AI for content. Inject the voice guide as a system prompt in every content generation call.
rule.rationale: Brand voice drift occurs when multiple AI sessions produce content independently without a consistent system prompt. "Conversational but authoritative" means nothing without examples; specific examples produce consistent output.

text.reference: Pre-publish checklist: generic signals to remove, differentiation signals required
Template for adding E-E-A-T signals to existing content
